agent_outputs.raw: Three simultaneous operational changes — tax-refund season, new dialer vendor, and manager overrides — prevent any causal attribution from a before-after comparison. The model may or may not contribute; the existing evaluation cannot say. A concurrent champion-challenger design with documented override rates and stable dialer policy would isolate the model's contribution.

Tax-refund season (Feb–Apr) is the most significant single confound: it drives voluntary payments upward regardless of which accounts are prioritized. The dialer change adds a second, independent confound by changing the composition of the workable queue. Manager overrides add a third. Together these make the board memo's attribution claim unsupported. The memo should present the result as a promising signal requiring controlled follow-up.

The before-after design fails to control for four separable issues: seasonality, dialer-driven queue composition change, manager override contamination, and absence of a concurrent control. The 16% number is a real operational result but is not attributable to the model without a concurrent champion-challenger comparison. This is an empirically resolvable question — it requires a properly designed test, not more argument.

synthesis: Three independent assessments unanimously identify the evaluation as causally uninterpretable due to confounded design. All four issues (seasonality, dialer change, overrides, no control) are identified across at least two of three assessments. The question is genuinely empirically resolvable through a concurrent champion-challenger design.
all_issues_raised: dialer contactability change alters queue composition

empirical_test.condition: Concurrent randomized champion-challenger test within same calendar period, same dialer, documented overrides by condition
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: No material recovery difference between model-ranked and legacy-ranked concurrent conditions
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Model-ranked condition shows materially higher recovery in concurrent test
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Small positive model effect; majority of 16% attributable to seasonal and dialer factors
